triples.head: cells
triples.evidence: cells convert the chemical energy stored in glucose into ATP

triples.head: cells
triples.evidence: cells convert the chemical energy stored in glucose into ATP

triples.head: aerobic respiration
triples.evidence: In aerobic respiration, oxygen is the final electron acceptor

triples.head: glycolysis
triples.evidence: It occurs in the cytosol

triples.head: glycolysis
triples.evidence: one molecule of glucose is broken down into two molecules of pyruvate

triples.head: glycolysis
triples.evidence: Glycolysis produces a net gain of ATP

triples.head: glycolysis
triples.evidence: Glycolysis reduces NAD⁺ to NADH

triples.head: pyruvate
triples.evidence: pyruvate is transported into the mitochondrion

triples.head: pyruvate
triples.evidence: pyruvate is converted into acetyl-CoA

triples.head: pyruvate
triples.evidence: pyruvate is converted into acetyl-CoA, releasing carbon dioxide

triples.head: pyruvate
triples.evidence: pyruvate is converted into acetyl-CoA, producing additional NADH

triples.head: acetyl-CoA
triples.evidence: Acetyl-CoA enters the citric acid cycle

triples.head: citric acid cycle
triples.evidence: a series of reactions in the mitochondrial matrix that generates carbon dioxide

triples.head: citric acid cycle
triples.evidence: a series of reactions in the mitochondrial matrix that generates NADH

triples.head: citric acid cycle
triples.evidence: a series of reactions in the mitochondrial matrix that generates FADH₂

triples.head: citric acid cycle
triples.evidence: The citric acid cycle produces a small amount of ATP directly

triples.head: electron transport chain
triples.evidence: The electron transport chain (ETC) is located in the inner mitochondrial membrane

triples.head: NADH
triples.evidence: NADH and FADH₂ donate electrons to the ETC

triples.head: FADH₂
triples.evidence: NADH and FADH₂ donate electrons to the ETC

triples.head: electron transport chain
triples.evidence: their energy is used to pump protons (H⁺) from the matrix into the intermembrane space

triples.head: proton gradient
triples.evidence: This gradient stores potential energy

triples.head: ATP synthase
triples.evidence: ATP synthase uses the proton gradient to produce ATP

triples.head: ATP synthase
triples.evidence: the enzyme catalyzes the phosphorylation of ADP to ATP

triples.head: oxygen
triples.evidence: Oxygen accepts electrons at the end of the electron transport chain

triples.head: oxygen
triples.evidence: Oxygen accepts electrons at the end of the electron transport chain and combines with protons to form water

triples.head: electron transport chain
triples.evidence: If oxygen is not available, the electron transport chain cannot function

triples.head: proton gradient
triples.evidence: If oxygen is not available, the proton gradient collapses

triples.head: ATP production
triples.evidence: ATP production from oxidative phosphorylation decreases
